summary: Excellent Betty‑board capture of a raw, high‑energy 1978 monster – Bertha, Jack Straw and a raging Estimated Prophet/Eyes sequence dominate.
review: The tape is a pristine Betty board that delivers crystal‑clear instrument separation while preserving the chaotic, coke‑fuelled power of this June ’78 show.  Highlights include blistering renditions of Bertha, Jack Straw, Estimated Prophet into The Eyes and a motor‑bike‑revved Not Fade Away, though the second set’s over‑driven frenzy leaves some listeners feeling it runs out of control.  Overall the performance is celebrated for its wild intensity even as a few reviewers note sloppy moments and excessive screaming.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise both the soundboard recording (excellent) and the high‑energy performance, yielding a strong combined score; a minority of negative comments keep the overall rating just below top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty board source; reviewers repeatedly call it crisp, balanced and “perfect sound”; no major drop‑outs or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls with tight, hard‑rocking numbers; second set erupts into a chaotic, motorcycle‑revved jam that many find exhilarating but some deem over‑cooked and sloppy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone and solos (Bertha, Looks Like Rain, Estimated Prophet) are repeatedly praised; one reviewer notes an awkward transition during his Estimated solo.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocal screams on Around & Round draw both admiration for raw energy and criticism for being painful after repeated listens.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s synth envelope‑filter work is mentioned in context of the 1978 sound, but no clear performance judgment; Brent’s presence not discussed.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s drum segment (motorcycle revs, “snorting hogs”) is highlighted as a wild centerpiece of the jam.
